title: Facilities Officer/Town Area/Up to $4000
description:
Key Responsibilities:

Warehouse and Inventory Management

- Manage receiving, storage and issuance of equipment, engineering and facility-related materials, consumables, PPE, tools, and critical spares etc.
- Maintain accurate inventory records and stock levels using a digital inventory management system or manual logs.
- Conduct regular inventory audits and flag low stock or expired items.
- Coordinate with procurement staff and vendors to ensure timely replenishment of critical items.

M&E Engineering Support - Support daily facility inspections, troubleshooting and first-level fault diagnosis and reporting. - Monitor and document key readings from engineering systems (e.g. temperature, pressure, flow rates, filter differential pressures).

    Biosafety and WHS Coordination - Support the WHS Officer in implementing safety controls during maintenance or contractor works, including Permit-to-Work (PTW) and lockout/tag-out procedures. - Ensure work zones are kept in safe condition and assist with safety drills, inspections, and audits. - Report near-misses or safety concerns and follow up on corrective actions.
    Operational Readiness and Documentation - Assist in setup and testing of new equipment or infrastructure. - Maintain facility-related logs and records, including maintenance checklists, PTW forms, and service reports. - Escort contractors and vendors during facility works and ensure compliance with site safety rules.
    General Facility Support - Perform minor repairs, fixture replacements or workspace setups as needed. - Respond to facility fault reports (e.g. power trip, HVAC issue, temperature alarm). - Assist in general facility housekeeping, waste segregation logistics and pest control coordination etc.
    Qualification & Experience Requirements: Education & Experience: • Nitec (National ITE Certificate), Higher Nitec, and Technical Diploma in Mechanical, Electrical, Facilities or Building Services Engineering. • At least 2 years of relevant experience in technical facility operations. • Experience in inventory or store management is highly preferred.
    Key Skills & Attributes: • Able to understand basic engineering skill, building services (M&E), basic electrical/mechanical troubleshooting. • Able to use or learn basic inventory software/tools (e.g. Excel, barcoding, store systems). • A strong sense of safety, organisation and attention to detail. • Good communication and coordination skills.

    Eric Kng Chip Guan (CEI No.R1104559)
    Business Edge Personnel Services Pte Ltd (96C4864)
